title: Scale CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL to 3-node HA cluster with automated backups
subtasks: Update the CloudNative-PG Cluster CR to set instances: 3 (1 primary, 2 replicas) for high availability.
Configure synchronous replication with at least 1 synchronous standby.
Enable automated daily backups and WAL archiving to S3/R2 (use the same object storage as in dev, configure backup section in Cluster CR).
Set up pod anti-affinity to ensure replicas are scheduled on separate nodes.
Verify failover by simulating a primary pod failure and confirming automatic promotion.
Update the infra-endpoints ConfigMap if the service endpoint changes (e.g., to -rw/-ro services).

title: Scale Redis/Valkey to HA with Sentinel and configure maxmemory policy
subtasks: Update the Opstree Redis CR to enable Sentinel mode with 3 Redis replicas and 3 Sentinel pods.
Configure Sentinel quorum and failover timeouts for production.
Set maxmemory-policy to allkeys-lru and configure maxmemory based on production sizing.
Apply pod anti-affinity to spread Redis and Sentinel pods across nodes.
Update the infra-endpoints ConfigMap to point to the Sentinel-aware Redis service.
Test failover by killing the master pod and verifying Sentinel promotes a replica.

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN and TLS certificates for static assets and endpoints
subtasks: Set up Cloudflare DNS with proxied (orange cloud) records for all public domains.
Configure SSL/TLS mode to 'Full (Strict)' and enable 'Always Use HTTPS'.
Create cache rules for static asset paths (/_next/static/*, /assets/*) with long TTL and immutable cache headers.
Enable Brotli compression and minimum TLS 1.2.
Add security headers via Cloudflare Transform Rules (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options).
Request and manage TLS certificates for all domains via cert-manager (Let's Encrypt or Cloudflare Origin CA).

title: Configure Ingress controller and Cloudflare Tunnel for secure application routing
subtasks: Deploy or update the NGINX or Envoy Ingress controller with production-grade settings (timeouts, buffer sizes, rate limits).
Deploy cloudflared as a Deployment (2+ replicas) with tunnel credentials as a Kubernetes Secret.
Configure tunnel ingress rules to route morgan.<domain> and app.<domain> to the correct internal services.
Register DNS CNAMEs for tunnel endpoints in Cloudflare.
Ensure all ingress traffic is TLS-terminated and only accessible via Cloudflare Tunnel.
Test failover by killing a cloudflared pod and verifying zero downtime.

title: Apply Kubernetes NetworkPolicies to restrict inter-service traffic
subtasks: Create a default-deny-all ingress and egress NetworkPolicy in each application namespace.
Define allow-ingress policies for permitted service-to-service flows (e.g., API → PostgreSQL, API → Redis, web → API, Morgan → API).
Allow egress to kube-dns and required external APIs only.
Label all pods and namespaces for selector-based policies.
Test connectivity: verify allowed traffic works and denied traffic is blocked using test pods.
Document all NetworkPolicy manifests and communication flows.

title: Deploy Prometheus, Grafana, and Loki for observability with SLO alerting
subtasks: Deploy Prometheus (Helm or kube-prometheus-stack) with persistent storage and ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor for all /metrics endpoints.
Deploy Grafana with Prometheus and Loki as data sources, and import dashboards for application, database, and infrastructure metrics.
Deploy Loki and Promtail for centralized log aggregation from all pods.
Configure alert rules for SLO violations (e.g., error rate, latency, database replication lag).
Expose Grafana and Prometheus UIs internally or via Cloudflare Tunnel with authentication.
Verify metrics and logs are collected and dashboards display live data.

title: Configure OpenTelemetry Collector and Jaeger for distributed tracing
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a Deployment with appropriate resource limits.
Configure sampling rates for traces (e.g., 10% for production, 100% for errors).
Deploy Jaeger with persistent storage backend for trace retention.
Update application deployments to export traces to the OpenTelemetry Collector endpoint.
Verify traces are collected and visible in Jaeger UI.
Set up alerting for trace-based SLO violations (e.g., high latency, error traces).
